title: sagina nodosa subsp. borealis
intro: Sagina nodosa subsp. borealis, also known as Northern pearlwort, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the caryophyllaceae family, characterized by its small white flowers and rounded leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Sagina nodosa subsp. borealis include its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to act as an expectorant to help clear mucus from the lungs. Sagina nodosa subsp. borealis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health ailments.
